technique: Ransomware / destructive impact - recovery inhibition, encryption, service stop, resource hijacking

trigger.from_breadth: 4688 recovery-inhibition commands (vssadmin delete shadows, wmic shadowcopy delete, wbadmin delete catalog/systemstatebackup, bcdedit recoveryenabled no / bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures, cipher /w); OR a burst of service stops (7036/7040) hitting AV/backup/DB services; OR an ESET ransomware/encryptor detection; OR cryptominer strings (xmrig/kdevtmpfsi/kinsing). Any one of these is high-fidelity impact-stage activity.

telemetry_required: winlogbeat_winlog_event_id:(7036 OR 7040) for service stop/state change
ESET detection logs (ransomware/encryptor verdicts) ; Linux message for cryptominer strings
telemetry_gap_behavior: The actual file-encryption is BLIND without file-integrity monitoring / Sysmon - so the recovery-inhibition COMMANDS, service stops, ESET verdicts, and cryptominer strings are the highest-fidelity OBSERVABLE indicators. A shadow-copy deletion alone is near-certain ransomware staging -> treat as CRITICAL on its own; never wait for encryption you cannot see. Absent = KNOWN-GAP + recommend file-integrity monitoring.

method.query: winlogbeat_winlog_event_id:4688 AND winlogbeat_winlog_channel:Security AND message:("vssadmin" OR "shadowcopy delete" OR "wbadmin delete" OR "bcdedit" OR "recoveryenabled no" OR "bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures" OR "cipher /w")
method.decision_input: shadow-copy deletion / backup deletion / recovery disabled = ransomware staging -> CRITICAL

method.compute: burst of service stops on one host targeting AV (ESET), backup, SQL/DB, or VSS services
method.decision_input: mass stop of security/backup/DB services on one host = pre-encryption defense disable

method.compute: ESET ransomware/encryptor detections on the host; Linux/host cryptominer strings (xmrig/kdevtmpfsi/kinsing/sysrv, allow-list jndi/ceurufrd)
method.decision_input: ESET ransomware verdict = confirmed; cryptominer = T1496 resource-hijacking impact

method.compute: did this follow lateral movement / a compromise? how many hosts show the same indicators (spread)?
method.decision_input: recovery-inhibit/service-stop across MULTIPLE hosts after lateral movement = active ransomware deployment -> CRITICAL, escalate org-wide

decision.CRITICAL_conf5: shadow-copy/backup deletion or recovery disabled on any host, ESET ransomware verdict, or the same impact indicators across multiple hosts after lateral movement
decision.HIGH_conf4: a burst of AV/backup/DB service stops on one host, or an active cryptominer (T1496)
decision.MEDIUM_conf3: a single isolated VSS/bcdedit operation that may be legitimate maintenance - review the account + context
decision.FP_kill: backup software (Veeam/Commvault/Windows Backup) legitimately pruning shadow copies; admin running bcdedit during patching/recovery; sanctioned imaging tools - confirm the account + change window

false_positive_checks: backup products that manage/prune VSS shadows by design (confirm the binary + service account)
admin bcdedit / recovery changes during legitimate patching or system recovery (change window)
test/dev hosts running cipher /w for disk cleanup
Linux allow-list strings jndi / ceurufrd for the miner sweep

output_findings_json.query_template: winlogbeat_winlog_event_id:4688 AND message:("vssadmin" OR "shadowcopy delete" OR "wbadmin delete" OR "bcdedit")
